prompt_text: Hi. Welcome to my site. Here you will find exciting mashups of my very own recipes for the everyday home cook. I draw influences from my days working as an Executive Chef at my own Chinese restaurant in Downtown San Francisco, as well as inspiration from various cuisines I experience from all my travels.
base_text:
They also occasionally feature different menus on different evenings that focus on some kind of local traditional cuisine. Private candle light dinners can be arranged for couples at the comfort of their own suite on their own villa terrace, somewhere on the property or even special offsite location. Due to the seclusion of most Aman Properties, you won't be eating offsite much. Most meals are all had on the property. So for dinners we would change it up. We had a special dinner setup at a local villager's home where they cooked for us on the spot inside a tiny thatched hut with 100's of candles set all over the room. It was incredible.

Hi. Welcome to my site. Here you will find exciting mashups of my very own recipes for the everyday home cook. I draw influences from my days working as an Executive Chef at my own Chinese restaurant in Downtown San Francisco, as well as inspiration from various cuisines I experience from all my travels. Whether you're looking for quick weeknight dinners, unique fusion dishes, or comforting classics with a twist, there's something here for every palate. I believe cooking should be fun, approachable, and packed with flavor. So grab your apron and join me on this culinary journey—let’s explore new tastes together and bring a little adventure to your home kitchen!
final_text:
They also occasionally feature different menus on different evenings that focus on some kind of local traditional cuisine. Private candle light dinners can be arranged for couples at the comfort of their own suite on their own villa terrace, somewhere on the property or even special offsite location. Due to the seclusion of most Aman Properties, you won't be eating offsite much. Most meals are all had on the property. So for dinners we would change it up. We had a special dinner setup at a local villager's home where they cooked for us on the spot inside a tiny thatched hut with 100's of candles set all over the room. It was incredible.

Hi. Welcome to my site. Here you will find exciting mashups of my very own recipes for the everyday home cook. I draw influences from my days working as an Executive Chef at my own Chinese restaurant in Downtown San Francisco, as well as inspiration from various cuisines I experience from all my travels. Whether you're looking for quick weeknight dinners, unique fusion dishes, or comforting classics with a twist, there's something here for every palate. I believe cooking should be fun, approachable, and packed with flavor. So grab your apron and join me on this culinary journey—let’s explore new tastes together and bring a little adventure to your home kitchen!

label: ai
prompt_text:
Mornin' Hays

Sunday, June 7, 2015

I've been asked to post the speech I gave at senior celebration, the school awards ceremony held a few days prior to graduation. The video of me actually delivering the speech is MIA, so the best option I have is text.
base_text:
The constant search for resume-fillers are all part of a widespread misconception that I think too many high school students are falling victim to. For lack of a more creative phrase, it is just plain redundant. Most people I know who join NHS are exactly what the letter says they are: they receive good--sometimes outstanding--grades, they’ve impressed teachers, and they likely have been active in numerous extracurriculars. Those are some pretty good qualities to show off to colleges in and of themselves (even though they’re probably already fit to be accepted). For that reason, there really is no extra good that joining a group for no reason other than to name ONE MORE CLUB that, apparently, will automatically guarantee your admission into any school of your choice. Think about what you are already doing and how busy you already are before you try and force another extracurricular in, and only join clubs if you think they are meaningful and if you can gain something from them.

Mornin' Hays

Sunday, June 7, 2015

I've been asked to post the speech I gave at senior celebration, the school awards ceremony held a few days prior to graduation. The video of me actually delivering the speech is MIA, so the best option I have is text. Good evening, everyone. Teachers, families, friends, and most importantly, the graduating class of 2015.

Before I begin, I want to thank you all: for the laughter, the lessons, and the love that have carried us to this point. Some of you I’ve known for years, while others I just met this semester, but each of you has shaped my high school experience in ways I never could have imagined.
final_text:
The constant search for resume-fillers are all part of a widespread misconception that I think too many high school students are falling victim to. For lack of a more creative phrase, it is just plain redundant. Most people I know who join NHS are exactly what the letter says they are: they Receive good -- sometimes outstanding -- grades, they ’ ve impressed teachers, and they likely have been active in numerous extracurriculars. Those are some pretty good qualities to show off to colleges in and of themselves ( even though they ’ re probably already fit to be accepted ). For that reason, there really is no extra good that joining a group for no reason than to name ONE MORE CLUB that, apparently, will automatically guarantee your admission into any school of your choice. Think about what you are already doing and how busy you already are before you try and force another extracurricular in, and only join clubs if you think they are meaningful and if you can gain something from them.

Mornin' Hays

Sunday, June 7, 2015

I've ben asked to post the speech I gave at senior celebration, the school awards ceremony held a few days prior to graduation. The video of me actually delivering the speech is MIA, so the best option I have is text. Good evening, everyone. Teachers, families, friends, and most importantly, the graduating class of 2015.

Before I begin, I want to thank you all: for the laughter, the lessons, and the love that have carried us to this point. Some of you I’ve known for years, while others I just met this semester, but each of you has shaped my high school experience in ways I never could hase imagined.

KEYWORD “American Bodybuilding” – 17

So you’ve decided to try out American bodybuilding. Great! Now before we go right on to the basic starters in bodybuilding, let me first tell how bodybuilding came to America in the first place.
base_text:
For beginners in American bodybuilding, compound exercises are recommended. What makes compound exercises good is that it allows movement in two or more joints, thus allowing more muscle input. In American bodybuilding, especially when dealing with weights, you might want to go light at first to get a feel on how your muscle moves. There are countless other ways to consider when starting American bodybuilding but the ones stated above should get far enough. Related posts:

KEYWORD “American Bodybuilding” – 17

So you’ve decided to try out American bodybuilding. Great! Now before we go right on to the basic starters in bodybuilding, let me first tell how bodybuilding came to America in the first place. Bodybuilding as a formal discipline began gaining traction in the United States in the early 20th century, largely influenced by European strongmen and physical culture enthusiasts. The movement truly took off with pioneers like Eugen Sandow, who is often referred to as the "father of modern bodybuilding." Sandow toured America, demonstrating his physique and strength, inspiring many to pursue muscle development and fitness.

By the 1930s and 1940s, bodybuilding competitions started to become more popular, with the establishment of organizations such as the Amateur Athletic Union (AAU) promoting physique contests. However, it was in the post-World War II era that American bodybuilding exploded in popularity, thanks to figures like Steve Reeves and later, Arnold Schwarzenegger, who brought the sport into mainstream culture.

Today, American bodybuilding is a well-established sport with a huge following, numerous competitions like Mr.
final_text:
For beginners in American bodybuilding, compound exercises are recommended. What makes compound exercises good is that it allows movement in two or more joints, thus allowing more muscle input. In American bodybuilding, especially when dealing with weights, you might want to go light at first to get a feel on how your muscle moves. (CNN) — Sometimes, a massive typo is right in front of your eyes and you can't see it. For Hong Kong-based airline Cathay Pacific, it was between the "i"s -- on the livery of its Boeing 777-367. In a move that's had the Internet asking "WTF?" the airline revealed a new paint job on one of its flagship aircraft, but instead of the sleek, polished look fans expected, the plane bore a glaring error: the word "Cathay" was misspelled as "Cathayt." The extra "t" appeared just before the familiar "Pacific" logo, turning heads and sparking a flood of memes and jokes across social media platforms. Cathay Pacific quickly acknowledged the mistake and assured customers that it was a one-off error, promising a swift fix. Meanwhile, aviation enthusiasts and graphic design experts debated how such a slip could make it past multiple layers of quality control, turning this typo into an unexpected viral sensation.

prompt_text: The first adventure of the Orc War-series clocks in at 25 pages, 1 page front cover, 1 page editorial, 1 page SRD, leaving us with 22 pages of content, so let’s take a look! Now, before we take a look at the adventure, let us first talk about the supplemental material herein: The pdf devotes 7 pages of its content to 1st-level pregens, all of which sport decent full-color artworks as map tokens. They come with 2 pretty generic roleplaying traits and a “negative” – a roleplaying quirk, if you will.
base_text:
...

..

. All right, still here? The little Jaggard Isle, setting of this module, sports one city, one village and a couple of hamlets “The West Coast holds the ruins of a long lost population.” [sic!] – I am pretty sure that’s supposed to be “civilization.” The adventure begins as the PCs are awaiting their breakfast in the Mourning Shade Valley Hamlet – which would be as well as place as any to note that the pdf sometimes capitalizes things that shouldn’t be capitalized – like races, classes, etc. The PCs are told to go to a remote farm, meet up with a deputy and secure the place...killing all orcs they encounter. While the pdf does have (useless) information to be gleaned via interrogation of defeated foes, there is no reward for not killing foes...and the bounty is on orc hands, so yeah – murderhobo-ing.

The first adventure of the Orc War-series clocks in at 25 pages, 1 page front cover, 1 page editorial, 1 page SRD, leaving us with 22 pages of content, so let’s take a look! Now, before we take a look at the adventure, let us first talk about the supplemental material herein: The pdf devotes 7 pages of its content to 1st-level pregens, all of which sport decent full-color artworks as map tokens. They come with 2 pretty generic roleplaying traits and a “negative” – a roleplaying quirk, if you will. This approach is a nice touch, as it gives players a bit of personality to latch onto without overwhelming them with complex backstories. The pregens cover the typical archetypes you'd expect for a first-level party—fighter, rogue, cleric, wizard, and a ranger—providing a well-rounded group ready to tackle the adventure. Each character sheet is clearly laid out, with all necessary stats and abilities easily accessible, making them perfect for both new and experienced players who want to jump straight into the action.

Moving on to the maps and handouts, the adventure includes a handful of simple yet effective visuals. The main dungeon map is presented in a clean, black-and-white line style that is easy to read and navigate.
final_text:

Each character sheet is clearly laid out, with all necessary stats and abilities easily accessible, making them perfect for both new and experiecced players who want to jump straight into the action.

Moving on to the maps and handouts, the adventure includes a handful of simple yet effective visuals. The main dungeon map is presented in a clean, black-and-white line style that is easy to read and navigate.

prompt_text:
569 P.2d 10 (1977)
279 Or. 457
Garry HELMER and Barbara Helmer, Husband and Wife, Appellants/Cross-Respondents,
v.
TRANSAMERICA TITLE INSURANCE CO., a Corporation, Defendant, and
John A. Nelson and Doreen H. Nelson, Husband and Wife, Respondents/Cross-Appellants.
base_text:
Thus, when defendants agreed to proceed with the closing on August 15 there was no modification of the agreement; rather, it was an attempt to carry out the agreement. Further, the failure of this attempt was no breach because, as said above, the agreement provided for just such difficulties. Similarly, defendants' objections to the interest charges, their alleged request to Transamerica not to record the deed until the interest problem was resolved, and Transamerica's later inability to complete the necessary paper work on time were all common closing problems *14 and all fell within the agreement as written. Given this interpretation of the agreement, the trial court's conclusion that the earnest money agreement controlled was correct and is affirmed, even though the court may have erred in relying on the statute of frauds to reach his conclusion.

569 P.2d 10 (1977)
279 Or. 457
Garry HELMER and Barbara Helmer, Husband and Wife, Appellants/Cross-Respondents,
v.
TRANSAMERICA TITLE INSURANCE CO., a Corporation, Defendant, and
John A. Nelson and Doreen H. Nelson, Husband and Wife, Respondents/Cross-Appellants. Supreme Court of Oregon

Argued and Submitted January 5, 1977.
Decided March 9, 1977.

Appeal and cross-appeal from a judgment of the Circuit Court for Multnomah County, which granted summary judgment in favor of defendants John A. Nelson and Doreen H. Nelson and denied plaintiffs' (appellants) motion for summary judgment. The case involved a dispute over title insurance coverage and the validity of a certain deed.

For appellants: Garry Helmer argued pro se.

For respondents: John A.
final_text:
Thus, when defendants agreed to proceed with the closing on August 15 there was no modification of the agreement; rather, it was an attempt to carry out the agreement. Further, the failure of this attempt was no breach because, as said above, the agreement provided for just such difficulties. Similarly, defendants' objections to the interest charges, their alleged request to Transamerica not to reocrd the deed until the interest problem was resolved, and Transamerica's later inability to complete the necessary paper work on time were all common closing problems *14 and all fell within the agreement as written. Given this interpretation of the agreement, the trial court's conclusion that the earnest money agreement controlled was correct and is affirmed, even though the court may have erred in relying on the statut of frauds to reach his conclusion.

prompt_text: A three-month consultation period to discuss the move, which would outlaw the anchoring method where a player hinges a putter to a fixed point on the body, ends on Thursday. The PGA Tour, professional golf's biggest and most lucrative tour, has already written to the governing bodies to voice its opposition to the proposals unveiled in November last year.
base_text:
Keegan Bradley was the first player to implement the anchoring method to win a major when he claimed the 2011 US PGA. "The thinking of the players and our board of directors, and others that looked at this, was the absence of data to conclude there is a competitive advantage to be gained by using anchoring," Finchem said. "Given the amount of time that anchoring has been in the game, there was no overriding reason to go down that road. "The USGA did, on multiple occasions, look at this and came to one conclusion - 25 or 30 years later they have come to another conclusion. "They have asked us to give our comments. All we are doing at this point is saying 'this is our opinion'."

A three-month consultation period to discuss the move, which would outlaw the anchoring method where a player hinges a putter to a fixed point on the body, ends on Thursday. The PGA Tour, professional golf's biggest and most lucrative tour, has already written to the governing bodies to voice its opposition to the proposals unveiled in November last year. The proposed rule change has sparked intense debate among players, officials, and fans alike. Supporters argue that banning anchoring will preserve the traditional skill and integrity of the game, while critics contend that the move unfairly targets players who have relied on the technique to combat putting difficulties. Several top-ranked golfers have publicly expressed their concerns, warning that the ban could alter the dynamics of competitive play and affect their performance. The governing bodies are expected to review all feedback carefully before making a final decision, which will likely influence the future direction of professional and amateur golf worldwide.
final_text:
Kegan Bradley was the first player to implement the anchoring method to win a major when he claimed the 2011 US PGA. "The thinking of the players and our board of directors, and others that looked at this, was the absence of data to conclude there is a competitive advantage to be gained by using anchoring," Finchem said. "Given the amount of time that anchoring has been in the game, there was no overriding reason to go down that road. "The USGA did, on multiple occasions, look at this and came to one conclusion - 25 or 30 years later they have come to another conclusion. "They have asked us to give our comments. All we are doing at this point is saying 'this is our opinion'."

The PGA Tour, professional golf 's biggest and most lucrative tour, has already written to the governing bodies to voice its opposition to the proposals unveiled in November last year. The proposed rule change has sparked intense debate among players, officials, and fans alike. Supporters argue that banning anchoring will preserve the traditional skill and integrity of the game, while critics contend that the move unfairly targets players who have relied on the technique to combat putting difficulties. top-ranked golfers have publicly expressed their concerns, warning that the ban could alter the dynamics of competitive play and affect their performance. The governing bodies are expected to review all feedback carefully before making a final decision, which will likely influence the future direction of professional and amateur golf worldwide.

title: '**Thin sets of integers in Harmonic analysis and $p$-stable random Fourier series**'

Primary: 43A46 ; secondary: 42A55; 42A61; 60G52

Fourier series; quasi-independent set; Rider set; stable random variables; stationary set

Introduction
============

Let $G$ be a compact, abelian group (which will be mostly the circle ${\mathbb T}$), equipped with its normalized Haar measure $m$, and $\Gamma$ its (discrete) dual. We will denote by ${\cal P}$ the set of finite sums $\sum_{\gamma\in\Gamma} c_{\gamma}\gamma$, i.e.
base_text:
This is precisely the aim of this work, where we will be interested in the complex, symmetric, $p$-stable random variables $Z$, $1 < p < 2$, which can be defined through their characteristic function: $$\qquad\qquad
{\mathbb E}\,\big({{\rm e}}^{i{\rm R}e\,\overline{z}Z}\big) = \exp(-\vert z\vert^p),\quad \forall z\in{\mathbb C}.$$

The case $p=2$ is the gaussian case already studied. The case $1 < p < 2$ is in some sense more delicate, because in spite of the nice *stability* property: $$\sum_{n=1}^N a_n Z_n \sim \Big(\sum_{n=1}^N\vert a_n\vert^p \Big)^{\frac{1}{p}} Z_1$$ from which those variables borrow their name, their integrability properties are fairly poor: $Z \in L^s$ for each $s < p$, but $Z\not\in L^p$ (in fact, $Z\in L^{p,\infty}$). Yet, this case has also been studied in great detail by Marcus and Pisier in [@MaPi], who in particular introduced the following $p$-stable norm on the space of trigonometric polynomials $$\label{1.8}
\qquad \qquad
[\![f]\! ]_p = {\mathbb E}\, \Big\Vert \sum_{\gamma\in\gamma} 
Z_\gamma \hat f (\gamma)\gamma \Big\Vert_\infty,\quad \forall f\in{\cal P},$$ where $(Z_\gamma)_\gamma$ is a family of independent copies of a complex $p$-stable, symmetric, random variables. Observe that this has a meaning, since the $Z_\gamma$’s are integrable. Moreover, due to a general comparison principle of Jain and Marcus ([@JaMa]), one has the following inequality, where the implied constants only depend on $p_1$ and $p_2$: $$\label{1.9}
\qquad \quad
1< p_1 < p_2\leq 2 \quad\Rightarrow \quad [\![f]\! ]_{p_2}\leq C (p_1,p_2)\, [\![f]\!
